description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg, consensus finding F6 [Medium; spec+testing]. Sync.entries/2 halts the whole sync on the first invalid path or undecodable/future-schema entry ({:halt, {:error, ...}}) — one bad blob on the shared orphan branch (foreign tool write, future schema_version from a newer specled on a teammate's machine, manual damage) breaks evidence sync for EVERY peer until manually repaired. Skip-and-warn (carry unknown entries through untouched, or quarantine) instead of halting; spec the tolerance behavior in evidence_store.spec.md.
Files: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex:177-194; lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex:42-54.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W2)
Note: specled_-737 has landed on the epic branch — sync.ex is now rewired onto the shared SpecLedEx.Evidence.Git module; line numbers in the ticket body predate that refactor.
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (spec the tolerance behavior, as the ticket requires)
- test/specled_ex/evidence/** (sync/entry tests)
Out of scope (intent): one anomalous blob (invalid path, undecodable entry, future schema_version, foreign write) must no longer halt sync for every peer — skip-and-warn or quarantine, carrying unknown entries through untouched. Preserve the ADR safety property: evidence never gates; misses fail safe. Do not touch tree_hash.ex/git.ex (owned by specled_-43y this wave) beyond what compilation requires — disclose any such touch in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: (not assigned in delegation prompt)
Log: (not provided)

Commit: fdaac42
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest "quarantines an invalid path, unparsable JSON, and a future schema version without halting"; SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest "quarantined entries independently written at the same path converge deterministically"; SpecLedEx.Evidence.EntryTest "distinguishes malformed JSON and unsupported schema versions from other decode failures". Adapted (not deleted): SpecLedEx.Evidence.EntryTest "validates entry filenames on read" now asserts the new distinguishable reasons (:invalid_filename, :tree_hash_mismatch) instead of the old opaque :invalid_entry, preserving the same load-bearing "bad path fails" / "tree_hash mismatch fails" checks.
Verification stubs flipped: none flipped false->true; specled.evidence_store.sync_entry_tolerance was added directly to the existing execute:true command-kind stub (`mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs`).
Verification tier: change-scoped -- mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs test/specled_ex/evidence/store_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs (18 tests, 0 failures); mix test --stale (997 tests/3 properties, 0 failures); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0); mix format --check-formatted and mix deps.unlock --check-unused both clean. Full command-execution spec.check proof deferred to the stage merge gate.
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Sync.entries/2 used to halt reconciliation for every peer on the first invalid path, undecodable JSON, or future/unsupported schema_version entry on the shared spec-evidence orphan branch. Sync now quarantines any single entry it cannot validate -- carried through the tree union byte-identical under its original path, with a warning naming the path and reason in the result's new `warnings` field -- while every other entry still reconciles normally; same-path quarantine conflicts between independently-created peers resolve deterministically (max of raw bytes) so convergence still holds. Entry.decode_file/2 now returns distinguishable reasons (:invalid_filename, :tree_hash_mismatch, :malformed_json, {:unsupported_schema_version, v}, :missing_schema_version) instead of one opaque :invalid_entry. Added specled.evidence_store.sync_entry_tolerance (must) plus a scenario to evidence_store.spec.md. Note: printing warnings via `mix spec.sync`/`mix spec.prune` CLI output was left undone since lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex and lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex are outside this ticket's Allowed touches -- Sync.run/2 already returns the data (matching the existing Store.record/2 warning-tuple convention), and I opened standalone follow-up specled_-6j7 to wire the two task call sites.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs) across one commit fdaac42
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (tree_hash.ex and git.ex untouched, as required)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 execute:true command stub (the sync_entry_tolerance-covering `mix test sync_test.exs spec_sync_task_test.exs spec_prune_task_test.exs`), passed: 1 (18 tests incl. entry/store, 0 failures)
- Requirements coverage:
  - F6 one-bad-blob-no-longer-halts: covered by sync_test.exs "quarantines an invalid path, unparsable JSON, and a future schema version without halting". Seeds 3 anomalous blobs (non-hash path, garbage JSON, schema_version 2) then asserts {:ok, result} with action :pushed. Would fail if the pre-fix {:halt, {:error, {:invalid_evidence_path,...}}} returned — Sync.run would yield {:error,...} and the {:ok, result} match would blow up. Also asserts the valid entry still reconciles AND each bad blob survives byte-identical via cat-file on A and again after B adopts. Seams: :sleep injection (existing), inject_raw_entry uses real git plumbing (no SUT mocking).
  - Convergence with quarantined entry: covered by sync_test.exs "quarantined entries independently written at the same path converge deterministically". A and B independently write garbage at the same path; after A,B,A syncs both peers converge to Enum.max(raw bytes). Would fail if same-path raw resolution were non-deterministic (the two cat-file asserts on A and B diverge). merge_entry {:raw,:raw} -> Enum.max is commutative, so order-independence holds by construction. Seams: none problematic.
  - No data destruction / future-schema survives older peer: covered by the same first test — the schema_version 2 blob (a newer peer's write) is carried through A's push and B's adopt byte-identical, never dropped or rewritten.
  - Evidence still never gates: nothing in the diff converts quarantine into an error; warnings are returned data only, and the sync-exhaustion/error paths are unchanged. attestation_never_gates requirement and scenario untouched.
  - Distinguishable decode reasons: covered by entry_test.exs new "distinguishes malformed JSON and unsupported schema versions..." plus the adapted "validates entry filenames on read" (now :invalid_filename / :tree_hash_mismatch). Adapted assertions remain load-bearing — bad path and hash mismatch still must fail; if validation vanished, decode_file would return {:ok,...} and every assert breaks.
  - Spec addition: sync_entry_tolerance requirement (must) + sync_quarantines_bad_entries scenario added and wired into the command verification stub. No unrelated requirement statements were tightened (diff is additive only).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
- Targeted verification: PASS. `mix test sync_test.exs spec_sync_task_test.exs spec_prune_task_test.exs entry_test.exs store_test.exs` (18 tests, 0 failures); `mix test --stale` (9 tests, 0 failures); `mix format --check-formatted` clean; `mix deps.unlock --check-unused` clean; `mix spec.check --no-run-commands` status=pass errors=0 warnings=0.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Deferred-work disclosure: SOUND. CLI surfacing of the quarantine warnings is deferred to specled_-6j7 (confirmed present and open). Traced the code, not the claim: push_result merges `warnings: warnings` into every success result, and both task call sites bind the map (spec.sync.ex:30 `{:ok, result}`, spec.prune.ex:29 `{:ok, result}`), so the data is reachable by callers, not dropped internally — the tasks simply do not print result.warnings yet. Since lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex and spec.prune.ex are outside this ticket's Allowed touches, warnings-as-returned-data satisfies "warn" for THIS ticket.
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- Same-path known-vs-raw resolution keeps the :known side (merge_entry clauses). It is deterministic and order-independent, and only triggers when two peers independently minted evidence for the same tree at different schema versions — a reasonable choice, not tested directly.
- The convergence test exercises a single push order (A,B,A) rather than both; order-independence for quarantined entries follows from Enum.max being commutative, and the existing sync_converges_in_both_push_orders scenario already covers both-order convergence for valid entries. No gap.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub and requirement coverage, zero
scope violations, passing targeted verification, complete seam disclosure, and
no seam substituting production wiring. Deferred CLI warning surfacing is
disclosed with open follow-up specled_-6j7 and the warnings data path was
traced to real call sites. No auditor was spawned per policy.

title: review-F6: one anomalous evidence blob wedges sync for every peer
